mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2009-06-01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and higher corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during NBER-defined expansion periods, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor market conditions and rising wage income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll increases show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 215.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand, reducing recession risk and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day horizons, or interaction term significance vanishes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of monetary tightening signals stagflationary supply constraints, which erode corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles do not predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 45% in out-of-sample validation.
